data.title: Philip Morris International shares climb seven straight sessions
data.description:
Philip Morris International (PM) shares clocked seven straight sessions of gains, as the stock rose 0.7% at $173.86 on Friday.

The tobacco giant gained 8.7% in the preceding six sessions. The stock surged over 32% in the year 2025, compared to a 16.7% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

PM is up 10% over the past one month.

Most recently, Philip Morris highlighted that the company is strongly committed to investing in America, with over $20B of investments made since 2022, the majority of which were noted to be made to acquire and further invest in U.S. manufacturing capabilities, commercial rights and infrastructure, and U.S. jobs.

"Philip Morris International (PM) remains attractive due to robust dividend growth and strong positioning in the smoke-free segment," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, PM has a Buy rating with a score of 3.9 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, while it received D+ in valuation.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 14 analysts gave PM a Buy and above rating. Four analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while none recommended Sell.

 

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.

data.title: U.S. stocks seesaw, even as some chipmakers advance
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday searched for direction, with losses in utility stocks offset by gains in some chipmakers. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) struggled for momentum.

The Nasdaq (COMP:IND) was last +0.06% in afternoon trading, while the S&P (SP500) rose +0.12%. The blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) edged up +0.07%.  

Chipmakers extended their gains from the previous session in which they were buoyed by strong results from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and bargain hunting. Micron (MU) +5.87%, Lam Research (LRCX) +2.45%, and AMD (AMD) +1.74% were among the top percentage gainers on the Nasdaq (COMP:IND).

Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed 5.5 basis points to 4.23%, and the U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 3 basis points to 3.61%. The longer-dated U.S. 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) increased 4.3 basis points to 4.84%.

Top S&P 500 gainers included Super Micro Computer (SMCI) +9.62%, Micron (MU) +5.87%, and GE Vernova (GEV) +5.47%. Decliners included Constellation Energy (CEG) -11.52%, Vistra (VST) -8.26%, and Amcor (AMCR) -7.19%.

data.title: Dominion wins court OK to resume Virginia offshore wind project stopped by Trump
data.description:
A U.S. District Court judge ruled Friday that Dominion Energy (D) can resume work on its Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project while the company continues its legal challenge of the Trump administration's order to stop the $11.2B development.

The order issued by the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Virginia granted Dominion's (D) request for a preliminary injunction blocking the federal government from enforcing its stop-work order after the company said it was suffering irreparable harm and losing millions of dollars every day the project sits idle.

The ruling was the third legal defeat this week to President Trump's effort to stop offshore wind development, and the Virginia judge echoed concerns expressed in the other cases, saying the government had failed to show that any national security risk should halt the Dominion (D) project and that the government's order likely was arbitrary and capricious.

Dominion (D) already has spent nearly $9B on the Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project, which is expected to provide enough energy to power 600K homes.

data.title: Honeywell shares uptick for seven consecutive sessions
data.description:
Honeywell International (HON) shares clocked seven straight sessions of gains, as the stock rose 2% at $219.37 on Friday.

The industrial conglomerate gained nearly 5% in the preceding six sessions. The stock slipped over 13% in the year 2025, compared to a 16.7% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

HON is up 11% over the past one month.

Most recently, Honeywell (HON) confirmed that it intends to file for an IPO of its quantum unit Quantinuum, which was last valued at $10B, with offering details yet to be set.

"Quantinuum’s spinoff will clean up HON’s financials by removing a loss-making segment, supporting clearer reporting and strategic capital redeployment," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

Earlier in the day, J.P. Morgan upgraded shares and raised the price target on HON, saying the stock has extended its discount to a sum-of-the-parts valuation, comfortably off its highs compared to SOTP peers that are up a weighted 20%.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, HON has a Hold rating with a score of 2.9 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, while it received D in growth.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 12 analysts gave HON a Buy and above rating. 13 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while one recommended Sell.

Seeking Alpha analysts are cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

data.title: Regions Financial outlines 2026 net interest income growth target of 2.5%–4% as loan pipelines strengthen
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Regions Financial Corporation (RF) Q4 2025

Management View

  • John Turner, President, CEO & Chairman, announced a significant leadership change, stating, "I'd like to take a moment and personally thank David Turner for his service and leadership... I'm genuinely excited about working closely with Anil Chadha, our newly appointed CFO." Turner reported, "This morning, we reported strong full year earnings of $2.1 billion, resulting in earnings per share of $2.30 or $2.33 on an adjusted basis." He highlighted the company achieved "one of the highest returns on tangible common equity in the industry at just over 18%." For the fourth quarter, "We also reported solid fourth quarter earnings of $514 million, resulting in earnings per share of $0.58 and $0.57 on an adjusted basis." Turner emphasized ongoing technology modernization, stating, "We launched a new native mobile app that's performing exceptionally well, earning a 4.9 out of 5-star rating in the App Store." He also noted, "Loan growth was challenged in 2025... our net interest income continued to benefit from fixed asset turnover and prudent funding cost management, and we expect those tailwinds to persist."
  • David Turner, Senior EVP & CFO, detailed one-time items: "We recorded $26 million of incremental tax expense associated with adjustments to certain state income tax reserves," and "incurred a total of $14 million of incremental expense related to severance, pension settlement and Visa Class B litigation escrow funding. These items collectively reduced our fourth quarter EPS by $0.04."
  • Anil Chadha, Head of Corporate Finance & Controller, shared, "Our goal is to always invest in loan growth. And when we see that good quality loan growth, we're going to step in there and generate and invest capital into that."

Outlook

  • David Turner projected, "We expect net interest income to grow between 2.5% and 4%. The first quarter will be modestly lower, driven by fewer days and timing of HR-related asset dividends and the benefit from interest recoveries that benefited the fourth quarter. We anticipate sequential growth thereafter." He added, "After normalizing for nonrecurring items in the fourth quarter, a mid-360s net interest margin is a better starting point when looking to 2026."
  • For loans and deposits, Turner stated, "For the full year, we expect average loans to be up low single digits versus 2025... we again expect 2026 average deposits to be up low single digits versus the prior year."
  • On noninterest income, Turner provided, "For full year 2026, we expect adjusted noninterest income to grow between 3% and 5% versus 2025."
  • Adjusted noninterest expense is guided to be "up between 1.5% and 3.5%" for 2026.
  • Net charge-offs are expected "to be between 40 and 50 basis points" for 2026.

Financial Results

  • Regions reported "solid fourth quarter earnings of $514 million, resulting in earnings per share of $0.58 and $0.57 on an adjusted basis."
  • Net interest income "grew by 2% linked quarter at the upper end of our expected range." Net interest margin "rebounded to 3.7%, up 11 basis points, inclusive of the nonrecurring benefits from higher-than-anticipated seasonal HR-related asset dividends and credit-related interest recoveries."
  • Adjusted noninterest income increased 5% in 2025 but declined 6% versus the third quarter, with the decline attributed to "postponed M&A transactions and normal seasonality in loan syndication and securities underwriting activity."
  • Wealth Management delivered "record full year revenue and a fourth consecutive quarter of growth," while mortgage income increased 8% in 2025.
  • Service charges increased 4% in 2025, led by treasury management and growth in consumer checking and operating accounts.
  • Ending deposits were "up approximately $800 million, supported by strong customer acquisition and retention."
  • Annualized net charge-offs as a percentage of average loans increased to 59 basis points, while the allowance for credit loss ratio declined to 1.76%.
  • The company ended the quarter with an estimated common equity Tier 1 ratio of 10.8%, and returned $2 billion to shareholders through dividends and share buybacks in 2025.

Q&A

  • Ryan Nash, Goldman Sachs: Asked about loan growth composition and guidance. John Turner responded, "Customer sentiment is generally positive... We've seen nice increase in pipeline activity quarter-over-quarter, year-over-year, and we believe that's a catalyst for growth." Turner underscored, "We're guiding to lower single-digit loan growth, I think we feel good about how we're positioned today."
  • Nash followed up on balancing growth and returns. Turner stated, "There's no trade-off between growth and returns. We need to make sure that we're sound first, we're profitable second and that we're growing third."
  • Robert Siefers, Piper Sandler: Inquired about capital markets performance. David Turner explained, "Loan syndications have a little bit of seasonality there in the fourth quarter, came in a bit weaker than we had hoped, but we believe that can pick up in 2026... It just had a -- we just didn't have the fourth quarter where we wanted it to be. So we think we're going to rebound and feel very confident."
  • Siefers asked about deposit beta; Turner noted, "We think -- and our guidance has really centered around the mid-30% change."
  • Gerard Cassidy, RBC: Queried about loan refinancing in capital markets. Turner said, "The cost of capital was lower, they could borrow more cheaply, terms were potentially better... Probably a little earlier than it does sometimes."
  • Cassidy followed up on credit quality in risk portfolios. Turner commented, "Credit quality or the deterioration in credit quality peaked a couple of quarters ago... we do expect to continue to see improvement in credit quality."
  • John Pancari, Evercore: Asked about capital allocation and buybacks. Chadha responded, "Our #1 focus is to invest back into our business through good loan growth. When we see that, we're definitely going to fund that with capital. When we don't see that, we're going to step in and buy back shares."
  • Pancari followed up on M&A. Turner stated, "M&A is not -- depository M&A is not part of our strategy today."
  • Peter Winter, D.A. Davidson: Asked about market share risks. Turner replied, "We think it's an opportunity... we have the chance to grow and last year, about 40%... of our new commercial banking relationships were won in those markets."
  • Winter inquired about modernization benefits. Turner confirmed, "We have worked through all the very difficult integration work, and we've now entered the user testing phase... we think that will give us a speed to market flexibility as we're going to offer customers new products."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was generally positive, marked by congratulations to the outgoing CFO and interest in growth initiatives, but included probing on loan growth, capital markets, and competitive positioning. Analysts frequently sought clarification on growth prospects, loan pipeline momentum, and expense management strategies.
  • Management's sentiment was confident and disciplined, emphasizing execution, risk management, and clarity on strategic direction. John Turner frequently highlighted foundational strengths and future opportunities, using phrases like "we feel good about where we are and the opportunities ahead."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained confident but was more forward-looking, especially with the CFO transition and increased focus on technology investment and loan growth. Analyst tone was consistent, with continued scrutiny on loan growth and expense trends but less concern over credit quality deterioration.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for net interest income growth shifted from 3%–4% in Q3 to 2.5%–4% for 2026, with expectations for sequential growth after a softer first quarter.
  • Loan growth guidance moved from "relatively stable" in Q3 to "up low single digits" for 2026, reflecting increased confidence in loan pipelines and reduced runoff headwinds.
  • Expense guidance for 2026 (up 1.5%–3.5%) was slightly higher than the 2% increase expected for 2025, reflecting ongoing technology and banker investments.
  • Credit quality outlook improved, with net charge-offs guided to 40–50 basis points, down from a peak of 59 basis points in Q4.
  • Management's tone showed greater focus on upcoming technology transformations and talent acquisition, while analysts remained focused on growth levers and capital deployment.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged that "loan growth was challenged in 2025" due to capital markets refinancing and portfolio exits.
  • Expense pressures from technology investments and banker hiring remain, with guidance for higher technology spend (10%–12% of revenue).
  • Asset repricing risk persists as "asset repricing is exposed to [middle] and long-term rate fluctuations."
  • The company is mindful of potential headwinds in select industries, with Turner noting, "trucking and transportation is getting better, but still challenged from our perspective."
  • Analysts questioned cost management, the timeline for technology benefits, and the risk of further loan runoff.

Final Takeaway

Regions Financial Corporation delivered solid Q4 and full-year results, underpinned by disciplined execution, robust fee income growth, and ongoing technology transformation. With the CFO transition underway and strategic investments in talent and systems, management projects low single-digit loan and deposit growth, net interest income growth of 2.5%–4%, and continued improvement in credit quality for 2026. The company maintains a balanced approach to growth and capital deployment, positioning itself for sustainable long-term performance as it enters a new leadership era and advances its modernization agenda.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: OpenAI makes low-cost ChatGPT Go plan available worldwide; starts ad testing
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) said on Friday that its low-cost ChatGPT Go subscription plan is now available around the world, as the generative artificial intelligence company looks to boost growth. The company also confirmed its plan to introduce advertising into ChatGPT, further placing in competition with other tech giants, such as Google (GOOG) (GOGOL).

“In markets where Go has been available, we’ve seen strong adoption and regular everyday use for tasks like writing, learning, image creation, and problem-solving,” OpenAI said on its website. “This early momentum helped inform our decision to make ChatGPT Go available globally.” The plan, initially launched in August 2025, is now available in more than 170 countries and is OpenAI's “fastest growing plan and among the most affordable AI subscription globally.”

Included in ChatGPT is access to OpenAI's latest model, GPT-5.2 Instant, as well as 10 times more messages, uploads and image creation, when compared to the free version of ChatGPT. It also offers users longer memory and context window, OpenAI added.

ChatGPT Go is priced at $8 per month in the U.S., compared to ChatGPT Plus and ChatGPT Pro, which are priced at $20 and $200 per month, respectively.

Ads push

In addition, OpenAI said on Friday that it would start testing ads in the free tier and ChatGPT Go in the U.S. “soon.” Its ChatGPT Plus, Pro, Business and Enterprise plans will remain ad-free, the Sam Altman-led company added.

“The best ads are useful, entertaining, and help people discover new products and services,” OpenAI added. “Given what AI can do, we're excited to develop new experiences over time that people find more helpful and relevant than any other ads. Conversational interfaces create possibilities for people to go beyond static messages and links. For example, soon you might see an ad and be able to directly ask the questions you need to make a purchase decision.”

Last month, it was reported that OpenAI was making progress with its push to show advertising inside its near-ubiquitous chatbot.

OpenAI’s CEO of Applications Fidji Simo has been looking for someone to oversee monetization efforts, including bringing advertising to ChatGPT. It was reported in September that Simo had met with potential candidates, including some of her former colleagues from Facebook (META) to lead a new team that will bring ads to ChatGPT.

The push into advertising could further boost OpenAI's revenue, especially important as the company looks set to raise another significant funding round, perhaps gathering another $100B from investors. In November, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said the ChatGPT maker was doing “well more” than $13B in revenue and suggested it could hit $100B in revenue in 2027. OpenAI currently generates revenue from selling subscriptions to ChatGPT to consumers and businesses alike, as well as providing access to its application programming interface and other tools.

data.title: Netflix streaming turns 19: Remembering the day the iconic red DVD envelope became extinct
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) started streaming 19 years ago today when it launched its “Watch Now” feature with around 1,000 titles on January 16, 2007. At its inception, Netflix (NFLX) streaming had video quality that was lower than DVDs, and the standard $5.99-a-month disc plan initially bought subscribers just six hours of streaming per month.

 

 

Of course, the company made a revolutionary transformation by offering unlimited streaming at no additional cost to rental-disc subscribers in 2008, and by 2010 CEO Reed Hastings confirmed the company had evolved from its DVD-by-mail roots. Within another two years, Netflix (NFLX) was producing original content such as House of Cards and Lilyhammer, setting the stage for other streaming players like Apple (AAPL), Amazon (AMZN), HBO (WBD), Hulu (DIS), and Disney+ (DIS). Netflix (NFLX) is now estimated to have more than 315M total global subscribers, although the company no longer reports official tallies.

On January 16, 2007, Netflix's (NFLX) stock closed at $0.32 per share on a split-adjusted basis. On Friday afternoon, the streaming stock was swapping hands at $88.09 per share. 

Looking to the future, Netflix (NFLX) is due to report fourth-quarter earnings next week in what will be closely watched reports in the media sector.

Netflix is expected to post fourth-quarter EPS of $0.55 on revenue of $11.97 billion.

“With much still to prove, we think Netflix is positioning for substantial growth in global advertising, and that should not be overlooked,” said Wedbush analyst Alicia Reese, adding that the firm expects ad revenue to “become Netflix’s primary revenue driver in 2026, with significant opportunities in 2027.”

Seeking Alpha analysts and Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings consider the stock a Hold, while Wall Street analysts are bullish and have it slotted at Buy.

data.title: Genmab down after trial data for AbbVie-partnered Epkinly in B-cell lymphoma
data.description:
Genmab (GMAB) on Friday announced that its bispecific antibody epcoritamab, marketed as Epkinly in partnership with AbbVie (ABBV), extended patient survival without tumor progression in a late-stage trial for diffuse large B-cell lymphoma ((DLBCL)).

However, Genmab (GMAB) lost ~7% after the readout, while AbbVie (ABB) traded flat.

Epcoritamab is currently available in the U.S. under the FDA's accelerated approval pathway for DLBCL patients whose cancer has come back (relapsed) or failed to respond to two or more treatments (refractory).

Citing topline data from a Phase 3 trial called EPCORE DLBCL-1, the Danish biotech said that epcoritamab as a single agent also improved patients’ complete response rates, duration of response, and time to next therapy.

However, there was no statistically significant improvement for overall survival, the gold standard used to measure the efficacy of a cancer therapy. The 483-patient global trial enrolled R/R DLBCL patients who had received at least one prior line of therapy.

The injectable indicated a tolerability profile consistent with its known safety findings, Genmab (GMAB) said, adding that it will meet with global regulators to discuss the next steps for the program.
